# mlir-opt crash on shape.from_extents with ub.poison during --convert-math-to-libm pass

**Author:** Subway2023
**URL:** https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/177941
**Status:** Closed
**Labels:** mlir, crash, generated by fuzzer
**Closed Date:** 2026-02-28T11:49:35Z

## Body

# Description

mlir-opt version: 22.1.0-rc1

mlir-opt crashes when folding shape.from_extents on an ub.poison index value during the --convert-math-to-libm pass.

# Reproduce
location: If the **shape.from_extents** is commented out, the crash no longer occurs.

```mlir
module {
  func.func @main() {
    %0 = ub.poison : index
    %1 = gpu.dynamic_shared_memory : memref<?xi8, #gpu.address_space<workgroup>>
    smt.solver(%0, %1) : (index, memref<?xi8, #gpu.address_space<workgroup>>) -> () {
      ^bb0(%arg0: index, %arg1: memref<?xi8, #gpu.address_space<workgroup>>):
      smt.yield
    }
    smt.solver(%0, %1) : (index, memref<?xi8, #gpu.address_space<workgroup>>) -> () {
      ^bb0(%arg0: index, %arg1: memref<?xi8, #gpu.address_space<workgroup>>):
      smt.yield
    }
    %2 = shape.from_extents %0 : index
    return
  }
}
```
```
mlir-opt --convert-math-to-libm test.mlir
```
```
mlir-opt: /mnt/sdd1/mlir/tool/llvm-project-llvmorg-22.1.0-rc1/llvm/include/llvm/Support/Casting.h:566: decltype(auto) llvm::cast(From&) [with To = mlir::IntegerAttr; From = mlir::Attribute]: Assertion `isa<To>(Val) && "cast<Ty>() argument of incompatible type!"' failed.
PLEASE submit a bug report to https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/ and include the crash backtrace and instructions to reproduce the bug.
Stack dump:
0.      Program arguments: /mnt/sdd1/sbw/mlir/tool/llvmorg-22.1.0-install/bin/mlir-opt --convert-math-to-libm /mnt/sdd1/sbw/mlir/data/genProgram_0125_filtered/genProgram_0125_valid/252349_2135_6.mlir
 #0 0x00005db7712b9e32 llvm::sys::PrintStackTrace(llvm::raw_ostream&, int) (/mnt/sdd1/sbw/mlir/tool/llvmorg-22.1.0-install/bin/mlir-opt+0x252fe32)
 #1 0x00005db7712b65af llvm::sys::RunSignalHandlers() (/mnt/sdd1/sbw/mlir/tool/llvmorg-22.1.0-install/bin/mlir-opt+0x252c5af)
 #2 0x00005db7712b66fc SignalHandler(int, siginfo_t*, void*) Signals.cpp:0:0
 #3 0x0000706b37445330 (/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6+0x45330)
```
